algorithm: DSTU 4145-2002 digital signature, GF(2^163), polynomial basis
source: docs/papers/DSTU_4145-2002.pdf, Annex B.1 (Додаток Б.1, pages 18-19) - the standard's own worked example. Independently cross-checked byte-for-byte against oracles/bouncycastle-java/core/src/test/java/org/bouncycastle/crypto/test/DSTU4145Test.java test163() (a hardcoded KAT, not derived from this PDF) - every field below matches exactly, which is what makes this vector genuinely dual-sourced rather than a single by-eye transcription off a scan.
note: The worked example starts from an already-computed hash value H(T) rather than a literal message T, so this vector exercises everything from 'reduce the hash to a field element' onward, not message hashing itself (Kupyna is already verified separately). 'e' is the pinned ephemeral value used by the sign direction in the standard's example - a real implementation must only use this fixed value for KAT reproduction, never for actual signing (see DSTU4145Signer's ParametersWithRandom/TestRandomData usage in the Bouncy Castle oracle for the same convention). The verify direction (Q, hash, r, s -> valid) is the RNG-free check and the one to implement/test first; the sign direction additionally requires exposing a fixed-ephemeral seam.
